en: TO THE CHOIRMASTER. A PSALM OF DAVID. In you, O LORD, do I take refuge; let me never be put to shame; in your righteousness deliver me!
zh: 耶和華啊！我投靠你； 求你使我永不羞愧， 求你按著你的公義搭救我。

en: Incline your ear to me; rescue me speedily! Be a rock of refuge for me, a strong fortress to save me!
zh: 求你留心聽我，趕快拯救我； 求你作我堅固的磐石， 作拯救我的堅壘。

en: For you are my rock and my fortress; and for your name's sake you lead me and guide me;
zh: 因為你是我的巖石、我的堅壘； 為你名的緣故，求你帶領我，引導我。

en: you take me out of the net they have hidden for me, for you are my refuge.
zh: 求你救我脫離人為我暗設的網羅， 因為你是我的避難所。

en: Into your hand I commit my spirit; you have redeemed me, O LORD, faithful God.
zh: 我把我的靈魂交在你手裡， 耶和華信實的 神啊！你救贖了我。

en: I hate those who pay regard to worthless idols, but I trust in the LORD.
zh: 我恨惡那些信奉虛無偶像的人； 至於我，我卻倚靠耶和華。

en: I will rejoice and be glad in your steadfast love, because you have seen my affliction; you have known the distress of my soul,
zh: 我要因你的慈愛歡喜快樂， 因為你看見了我的困苦， 知道我心中的痛苦；

en: and you have not delivered me into the hand of the enemy; you have set my feet in a broad place.
zh: 你沒有把我交在仇敵的手裡， 你使我的腳站穩在寬闊之地。

en: Be gracious to me, O LORD, for I am in distress; my eye is wasted from grief; my soul and my body also.
zh: 耶和華啊！求你恩待我，因為我在患難之中； 我的眼睛因愁煩昏花， 我的身、我的心也都衰殘。

en: For my life is spent with sorrow, and my years with sighing; my strength fails because of my iniquity, and my bones waste away.
zh: 我的生命因愁苦耗盡， 我的歲月在歎息中消逝， 我的力量因罪孽衰退， 我的骨頭也枯乾。

en: Because of all my adversaries I have become a reproach, especially to my neighbors, and an object of dread to my acquaintances; those who see me in the street flee from me.
zh: 我因眾仇敵的緣故，成了眾人羞辱的對象， 在我的鄰居面前更是這樣； 認識我的人都懼怕我； 在街上看見我的，都避開我。

en: I have been forgotten like one who is dead; I have become like a broken vessel.
zh: 我被人完全忘記，如同死了的人， 我好像破碎的器皿，

en: For I hear the whispering of many- terror on every side!- as they scheme together against me, as they plot to take my life.
zh: 我聽見許多人的毀謗， 四周都有驚嚇； 他們一同商議攻擊我， 圖謀要取我的性命。

en: But I trust in you, O LORD; I say, "You are my God."
zh: 但是，耶和華啊！我還是倚靠你； 我說：“你是我的 神。”

en: My times are in your hand; rescue me from the hand of my enemies and from my persecutors!
zh: 我的一生都在你的手中； 求你救我脫離我仇敵的手，和那些迫害我的人。

en: Make your face shine on your servant; save me in your steadfast love!
zh: 求你用你的臉光照你的僕人， 以你的慈愛拯救我。

en: O LORD, let me not be put to shame, for I call upon you; let the wicked be put to shame; let them go silently to Sheol.
zh: 耶和華啊！求你不要使我羞愧， 因為我向你呼求； 求你使惡人羞愧，使他們在陰間靜寂無聲。

en: Let the lying lips be mute, which speak insolently against the righteous in pride and contempt.
zh: 那些逞驕傲，態度輕慢， 說話狂傲攻擊義人的， 願他們說謊的嘴唇啞而無聲。

en: Oh, how abundant is your goodness, which you have stored up for those who fear you and worked for those who take refuge in you, in the sight of the children of mankind!
zh: 耶和華啊！你為敬畏你的人所珍藏的好處， 為投靠你的人在世人面前所施行的恩惠， 是多麼豐盛。

en: In the cover of your presence you hide them from the plots of men; you store them in your shelter from the strife of tongues.
zh: 你把他們藏在你面前的隱密處， 免得他們陷在世人的陰謀裡； 又把他們保護在帳棚內，以免落在口舌的爭競中。

en: Blessed be the LORD, for he has wondrously shown his steadfast love to me when I was in a besieged city.
zh: 耶和華是應當稱頌的， 因為我在被圍困的城裡， 他就向我顯出他奇妙的慈愛。

en: I had said in my alarm, "I am cut off from your sight." But you heard the voice of my pleas for mercy when I cried to you for help.
zh: 至於我，我曾在驚恐中說： “我從你眼前被隔絕”； 可是我向你呼求的時候， 你還是垂聽了我懇求的聲音。

en: Love the LORD, all you his saints! The LORD preserves the faithful but abundantly repays the one who acts in pride.
zh: 耶和華的眾聖民哪！你們都要愛耶和華； 耶和華保護誠實的人， 卻嚴厲地報應行事驕傲的人。

en: Be strong, and let your heart take courage, all you who wait for the LORD!
zh: 所有仰望耶和華的人， 你們都要剛強，堅固你們的心。
